action_items.task: Conduct a home visit at the client's residence.
action_items.context: Required as part of standard probation conditions.

action_items.task: Provide the client with a heads-up before the first home visit.
action_items.context: To ensure the client's mother can lock up the dogs prior to the visit.

action_items.task: Bring a printed copy of the official school schedule.
action_items.context: To document the late Thursday lab that interferes with the 10:00 PM curfew.

action_items.task: Call the after-hours number and leave a voicemail if returning home past 10:00 PM.
action_items.context: Required if the bus runs late after Thursday night labs to avoid a curfew violation.

action_items.task: Report to the office on the first and third Wednesday of every month at 2:00 PM.
action_items.context: Twice-a-month reporting rotation required for the first 90 days.

action_items.task: Adhere to standard probation conditions: 10:00 PM to 6:00 AM curfew, remain in the county, no weapon possession, and keep up with education.
action_items.context: Standing obligations reviewed and signed during the meeting.

action_items.task: Submit a travel permit request at least two weeks before leaving the state.
action_items.context: Required for any out-of-state travel, such as the potential trip to visit family in Texas.

critical_updates.details: Client continues to live with his mother and stepdad at his residence on West 4th.
Client is enrolled in school full-time, taking 12 credits. He has a Thursday lab that runs until 9:30 PM. He reported failing two classes last semester and is currently focused on catching up.
Client is currently not working in order to focus entirely on his classes and passing school.
Client's stepdad is financially supporting him with food and living expenses so he can focus on his education. Family may be planning a trip to visit his abuela in Texas.

entities.value: first and third Wednesday of every month

next_episode_context: Client is currently stable and living with his parents on West 4th. He is not employed, choosing to focus full-time on his education (12 credits) after failing two classes last semester. He is on a twice-a-month reporting schedule. A 10:00 PM curfew is in effect, but he was instructed to bring his school schedule to the next appointment to document a Thursday lab that runs until 9:30 PM.
